summary: One test in DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed: the owner entity version was not incremented when a collection was modified, contrary to test expectations. This is the only failure among 16,923 tests run in the hibernate-core test suite.
root_cause: Test assertion failure at DbVersionTest.java:60. The test expected ownerVersionNotIncremented to be false (owner version should increment when collection changes), but it was true (owner version did not increment). Assertion: assertFalse(ownerVersionNotIncremented).

evidence.reason: Explicit statement of test failure in the execution log

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: Root cause of failure: assertion expected false but received true, indicating owner version was not incremented when it should have been
Identifies the exact test file and line number where assertion failed
Shows this is an isolated failure affecting only one test in a large test suite

suggested_fix: Investigate the collection version behavior in DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion at line 60 to determine whether: (1) the test expectation is incorrect and should be updated, or (2) the Hibernate ORM versioning behavior for collections has regressed and needs to be fixed in the version handling code

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
